title: Panicum pseudoneurachne
taxonomy: Panicum pseudoneurachne is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is a member of the genus Panicum, which comprises various species of grasses. Panicum pseudoneurachne is native to Australia and is often found in grasslands and savannas. The species is characterized by its dense growth habit and flat, narrow leaves. As a member of the Poaceae family, Panicum pseudoneurachne is closely related to other grasses and wild grasses.

intro: Panicum pseudoneurachne, also known as 'Native Millet', is a type of grass native to Australia, characterized by its slender stems and feathery panicles. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, as well as its ability to support immune function and reduce stress. Bioactive constituents of Panicum pseudoneurachne include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
